page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m1.medium is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.m1 server family. Built on the KVM hypervisor and the x86_64 CPU architecture, this instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores, resulting in a single thread per core configuration. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU hardware, requiring external block storage volumes for operating system and application data. It also does not feature complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Given its balanced 1:4 CPU-to-memory ratio, the ecs.m1.medium is designed for general-purpose workloads such as web serving, application hosting, and moderate database tasks that do not require specialized hardware acceleration or local NVMe storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m1.medium is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance within the ecs.m1 family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped directly to 4 physical cores with a single thread per core. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core ratio. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU accelerators, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Lacking complimentary public IPv4 addresses, this instance is structured for secure internal network deployments. The balanced resource allocation makes it suitable for general-purpose workloads, including mid-tier web servers, application hosting, and containerized microservices.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m1.medium is a general-purpose KVM virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for balanced workloads.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual instance featuring dedicated x86_64 cores and a stable one-to-four processor-to-memory ratio.
